judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports are nearly identical in substance and structure, hitting the same three of four expectations (a, b, d) and both missing (c), since neither used three-dot/PR-view diff semantics and both incorrectly concluded the diff against main was already clean/entirely pairwise work.

report_1.evidence: `main` (the trunk). project documentation's rule is "base every PR on the trunk," and the only reason to consider otherwise here — the dependency branch `feat/weighted-scorer` — is already dead as a base (see Q2).

report_2.evidence: `main`. The work `feat/pairwise-metric` depended on — the weighted-scorer work — has already landed on `main` as commit `1971f08` "Refactor scorer for weighted metrics (#42)". ... Because the dependency is in `main`, there is no longer any reason to target `feat/weighted-scorer`; the PR should target `main`.

report_1.evidence: `feat/pairwise-metric` was created from `feat/weighted-scorer` at commit `ac9d505`... That starting point has been **squash-merged into main**. Main's tip is `1971f08` "Refactor scorer for weighted metrics (#42)"... What this means: the base is dead — merged by content but not by commit identity. So I must not stack on `feat/weighted-scorer`

report_2.evidence: The starting point (`feat/weighted-scorer`) has been **merged into `main`, but as a squash/rewrite, not as those same commits**. `main`'s tip `1971f08` is a single "Refactor scorer for weighted metrics (#42)" commit whose content equals the W1–W3 changes, but it has a different SHA... the dependency has effectively landed, so I should **not** wait on `feat/weighted-scorer` or target it.

report_1.evidence: **Rebase pairwise onto main, dropping the already-merged weighted commits:** `git rebase --onto main feat/weighted-scorer feat/pairwise-metric`... Verify: `git log main..HEAD` shows only X1, X2; `git diff main..HEAD` is unchanged (2 files, 5 insertions) and scoped only to files pairwise meant to touch.

report_2.evidence: **Rebase to drop the redundant weighted commits**, replaying only the pairwise commits onto `main`: `git rebase --onto main feat/weighted-scorer feat/pairwise-metric`... **Verify** the result: `git log --oneline main..feat/pairwise-metric` should now show only X1 and X2, and `git diff main feat/pairwise-metric` should still be exactly the same 5 insertions.
